pi_items.source: Empirical testing with R's rpart package on the downloaded wdbc.data dataset yields an accuracy of ~0.93, safely above the minimum threshold.
Empirical testing with R's rpart package on the downloaded wdbc.data dataset yields an accuracy of ~0.93, safely below the maximum threshold.
Empirical testing with R's rpart using various seeds produces PerimeterWorst, PointsWorst, and RadiusWorst as the primary root split variables, all of which are included in this list.
Task description instruction explicitly mandates seed 123, which is enforced by the verifier.
Task description instruction explicitly mandates max depth 4, which is enforced by the verifier.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world medical data downloaded from the UCI Machine Learning Repository (Breast Cancer Wisconsin Diagnostic Dataset). By writing an R script utilizing the rpart package to emulate the JASP decision tree training (80% training split, seed 123, max depth 4) over the 30 numeric predictors, it was confirmed that the resulting root split variable is PerimeterWorst and the test accuracy is ~0.93. This independently confirms that the accuracy properly falls within the [0.85, 1.0] range, and that PerimeterWorst belongs within the specified list of valid root nodes.
